input.title: Multi-Vendor eCommerce Marketplace
input.description: Multi-Vendor eCommerce Marketplace is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Vendor Registration
requirements: Apply to become a seller with business details

FR-02.title: Vendor Store Setup
requirements: Customize storefront with logo and banner

FR-03.title: Product Listing
requirements: Add products with title, description, images

FR-04.title: Product Approval
requirements: Quality check for images and descriptions

FR-05.title: Multi-vendor Cart
requirements: Cart supports items from multiple vendors
Group items by vendor in cart

FR-06.title: Checkout with Split Payment
requirements: Payment split to vendors per commission

FR-07.title: Order Routing
requirements: Route order items to respective vendors

FR-10.title: Vendor Payouts
requirements: Payout to bank account or PayPal

FR-11.title: Product Search
requirements: Filter by price, rating, vendor, category

FR-13.title: Dispute Resolution
requirements: Buyer opens dispute for order issues

FR-17.title: Wishlist
requirements: Price drop notifications for saved items

FR-23.title: Inventory Sync
requirements: Vendor syncs inventory from external system

FR-24.title: Mobile App
requirements: Push notifications for offers and orders

FR-29.title: Bulk Operations
requirements: Vendor bulk product upload via CSV

FR-01.title: Design Vendor Registration UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for vendor registration. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for vendor registration is complete and reviewable.
The vendor registration design is responsive and accessible.
The design for vendor registration aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Vendor Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for vendor registration. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Apply to become a seller with business details; Document verification for business license; Multi-step onboarding wizard.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required vendor registration user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for vendor registration are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for vendor registration is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Vendor Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for vendor registration. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Apply to become a seller with business details; Document verification for business license; Multi-step onboarding wizard.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for vendor registration is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for vendor registration enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for vendor registration is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Vendor Registration
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for vendor registration. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for vendor registration supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for vendor registration are defined correctly.
Schema changes for vendor registration can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Vendor Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for vendor registration. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for vendor registration enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for vendor registration are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of vendor registration satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Testing Vendor Registration Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for vendor registration. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for vendor registration.
Security, validation, and permission checks for vendor registration are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important vendor registration behaviors.
